title: Anomalous thickness dependence of Curie temperature in air-stable two-dimensional ferromagnetic 1T-CrTe2 grown by chemical vapor deposition

paper_analysis.summary: This paper reports chemical vapor deposition growth of air-stable ultrathin 1T-CrTe2 single crystals on SiO2/Si with controllable thickness. The key findings are that the crystals show intrinsic ferromagnetism with robust anomalous Hall effect, a thickness-driven switch of magnetic easy axis from in-plane to out-of-plane, and an anomalous increase of Curie temperature as thickness decreases from ~130 nm to ~7.5 nm, which the authors attribute to weakened Coulomb screening in the 2D limit.

figures.description: Schematic of CVD growth of 1T-CrTe2 single crystals on SiO2/Si using CrCl2 and Te precursors.
Thickness of 1T-CrTe2 single crystals as a function of growth temperature, with optical microscopy images and a Raman mapping inset showing spatial uniformity.
figures.data_type: image / processed quantitative plot / Raman map

figures.description: AFM image and height profile of a representative hexagonal 1T-CrTe2 flake showing ~1.2 nm thickness.
Optical images and Raman intensity evolution of a ~4 nm sample exposed to air for 0, 5, 10, and 15 days, demonstrating air stability.
Low-magnification STEM-HAADF image of a hexagonal 1T-CrTe2 flake.
Atomic-resolution STEM-HAADF image showing the in-plane hexagonal atomic arrangement of 1T-CrTe2.
Fast Fourier transform pattern corresponding to the atomic-resolution STEM image.
Cross-sectional atomic-resolution STEM-HAADF image showing layered Z-shaped octahedral coordination in the 1T phase.
STEM-EELS elemental map of Cr.
Overlay of Cr-EELS map on HAADF image.
Magnetization hysteresis loop for ~40 nm 1T-CrTe2 with magnetic field parallel to the c-axis.
Magnetization hysteresis loop for ~40 nm 1T-CrTe2 with magnetic field perpendicular to the c-axis.
Magnetization hysteresis loop for ~3 nm 1T-CrTe2 with magnetic field parallel to the c-axis.
Magnetization hysteresis loop for ~3 nm 1T-CrTe2 with magnetic field perpendicular to the c-axis.
Remanence and coercive force versus thickness, extracted from 100 K hysteresis loops.
DFT-calculated magnetic anisotropy energy versus on-site Coulomb potential U for monolayer and bulk 1T-CrTe2.
Out-of-plane Hall resistance hysteresis loops and anomalous Hall signal for a 10 nm device.
Magnetoresistance versus magnetic field for the 10 nm device, showing butterfly-shaped hysteresis.
Temperature-dependent Hall resistance for a 15 nm 1T-CrTe2 crystal.
Temperature-dependent Hall resistance for a 130 nm 1T-CrTe2 crystal.
RMCD signal versus out-of-plane magnetic field at different temperatures for a 7.6 nm domain.
RMCD signal versus out-of-plane magnetic field at different temperatures for a 19 nm domain.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: The paper reports raw experimental measurements from OM, AFM, Raman spectra and Raman maps, XPS, STEM-HAADF images, EELS/EDS maps, VSM/SQUID magnetization loops, Hall and magnetoresistance transport traces, and RMCD field/temperature sweeps. Theoretical raw inputs are not provided as raw files, but DFT calculations were performed using experimental lattice constants and relaxed atomic structures.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: The data that support the study are available in this paper and corresponding Supplementary Information. Additional data are available from the corresponding authors upon reasonable request.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Raw measurement files are not explicitly described as downloadable or deposited in a repository. The paper states that supporting data are contained in the paper and Supplementary Information, but does not specify file formats, archives, or a public raw-data repository.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data include thickness-versus-growth-temperature statistics, Raman intensity maps, extracted remanence/coercive force versus thickness, Curie temperatures extracted from dM/dT minima, Arrott plots, RMCD remanence-derived Tc values, Hall resistance and magnetoresistance curves, and DFT-derived MAE and DOS plots. These are figure-ready quantitative summaries derived from measurements or calculations.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Figure-specific data are clearly presented in the main figures and Supplementary Figures, but the paper does not explicitly state that separate source-data files are provided. The figures appear to be built from experimental traces, maps, and extracted summary values shown directly in the article and supplementary material.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: No analysis scripts, notebooks, or code repositories are mentioned. DFT calculations are described methodologically, but no scripts are provided.
dataset_characterization.notes: The dataset is primarily a mixed experimental/theoretical figure dataset embedded in the article and supplementary information. The paper emphasizes multiple characterization modalities and thickness-dependent statistics, but does not describe a standalone data package. The most important semantic distinction is that many plotted quantities are processed/extracted from raw measurements: Tc from dM/dT minima or Arrott plots, remanence/coercivity from hysteresis loops, and transport-derived carrier density/mobility from Hall data. Theoretical outputs (MAE, DOS) are simulation results rather than measured data.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: The Methods section describes direct acquisition of OM, AFM, Raman, XPS, STEM, VSM/SQUID, RMCD, and transport measurements, implying raw instrument data were collected.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: The paper does not mention a separate source-data repository or explicit source-data files; it only states that data are available in the paper and Supplementary Information.
classification_prior.data_availability_section_relevant: “The data that support the study are available in this paper and corresponding Supplementary Information. Additional data are available from the corresponding authors upon reasonable request.”
